title: stapelia caespitosa var. hirtella
intro: Stapelia caespitosa var. hirtella, also known as the cluster-stemmed stapelia or hairy stapelia, is a succulent plant species within the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its unique, tubular flowers with a strong, unpleasant odor that is often mistaken for rotting flesh. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Stapelia caespitosa var. hirtella include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate various digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
